spec_match.rationale: The demo now drives each fragment through both the standards-mode document and a real no-doctype BackCompat iframe, matching the HTML fragment target selection contract.

interactive.rationale: The user can click exact and wrong-case fragments across lowercase, camel-case, and uppercase anchors, with live standards and quirks target readouts and an updating result matrix.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and support note now separate the Chrome 150+ expected exact-case behavior from older Chrome quirks fallback behavior observed in the current browser.

edge_cases.rationale: The page covers exact matches, wrong-case fragments, and three casing patterns, and labels Chrome <150 BackCompat case-insensitive fallback as fallback instead of success.

references.rationale: Cites the ChromeStatus entry and MDN links clearly.

design_system.rationale: Adapts to the platform showcase design system, using clear test-cards and distinct color-coded badges for success/failure.

summary: Repaired with Chrome DevTools MCP evidence. Before the fix the page was CSS1Compat only, had zero iframes, and relied on a static quirks table. After the fix, Chrome 148 showed a CSS1Compat parent plus a BackCompat iframe; exact fragments matched in both modes, wrong-case fragments had no standards target, and the BackCompat iframe showed pre-150 fallback targets that the page labels separately. Console had no errors, network requests were clean, and the shared conformance route rendered 7 PASS / 0 FAIL.
